---
applyTo: "**/*.md,**/*.mdx"
---

# OKR Coach Markdown Instructions

When editing or reviewing Markdown that contains OKRs, goals, quarterly priorities, planning notes, pilot metrics, or strategy docs, preserve the user's intended business context but rewrite weak OKRs into outcome-oriented quarterly OKRs.

Objectives must be singular, motivating, directional, and outcome-focused. Do not leave Objectives as tasks such as launching, implementing, delivering, creating, publishing, training, or improving communication.

Key Results must be measurable, observable, or evidence-based. They should answer "How will we know?" and should not be initiative lists.

If the OKR is a pilot, experiment, first-time capability, or weak-baseline area, use learning, adoption, usage, behavior change, stakeholder confidence, repeatability, proxy metrics, and decision-readiness instead of premature ROI or savings metrics.

When proposing replacements, include an audit note that names the anti-pattern and explains the outcome the task is trying to create.
